summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / press freedom, AI / privacy, AI / computing. The strongest items in this run are ‘Huge milestone’ as Libyan militia commander accused of torture to appear at ICC, Trump dismisses $10bn suit against IRS and creates $1.7bn ‘anti-weaponization’ fund, and Trump news at a glance: president says scheduled attack on Iran has been postponed – for now.

mainItems.title: ‘Huge milestone’ as Libyan militia commander accused of torture to appear at ICC
mainItems.summary: Prosecution is seen as landmark step towards justice over abuses of refugees trying to reach Europe from Africa A former militia commander accused of overseeing murder, rape, enslavement and torture in Libyan detention centres will appear…

mainItems.title: Trump dismisses $10bn suit against IRS and creates $1.7bn ‘anti-weaponization’ fund
mainItems.summary: Democrats criticize deal as a slush fund that ‘funnels taxpayer dollars’ to president’s political allies Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The justice department announced on Monday it was creating a loosely controlled and…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Touches trust, surveillance, and the private use of AI systems.

mainItems.title: Trump news at a glance: president says scheduled attack on Iran has been postponed – for now
mainItems.summary: President says he has instructed US military to be ready for ‘a full, large scale assault of Iran, on a moment’s notice’ – key US politics stories from Monday 18 May Donald Trump says he has called off a planned attack on Iran at the…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Here’s why Elon Musk lost his suit against OpenAI
mainItems.summary: On Monday, the jury in Musk v. Altman dealt Elon Musk a major blow—reaching a unanimous advisory verdict that he had sued OpenAI too late and, as a result, his claims are barred by the applicable statutes of limitations.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: High school students learn historic building techniques
mainItems.summary: The U.S. has many buildings that are centuries old, but most modern tradespeople haven't learned how to work on them. A New Hampshire program is teaching high schoolers these old restoration skills.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.title: Jury dismisses all claims in Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI CEO Sam Altman
mainItems.summary: A jury in a California courtroom has dismissed all claims in the case Elon Musk brought against Sam Altman and OpenAI.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Touches trust, surveillance, and the private use of AI systems.

mainItems.title: Opinion: Students Are Digital Natives. Let Them Lead the AI Revolution in Education
mainItems.summary: For 30 years, American public schools have lived through the era of education reform: standards, accountability, assessments and increased public investment tied to measurable results.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, student agency, and school policy.

mainItems.title: Researchers Wanted Preschool Teachers to Wear Cameras to Train AI

mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: Jury hands victory to Sam Altman and OpenAI in battle with Elon Musk
mainItems.summary: OpenAI CEO and president found not liable for breaking contracts made with Musk when founding the startup A jury ruled in favor of Sam Altman in the culmination of a long and bitter legal battle that pitted the richest person in the world…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: As the Stop Killing Games initiative advances, blocky racing game Lego 2K Drive is being delisted tomorrow
mainItems.summary: The Stop Killing Games initiative, which is fighting against the full termination of games, recently made it to the European Parliament and has pushed publishers like Ubisoft to make their games accessible when the servers are shut off -…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: This Ebola outbreak raises questions about when it all began — and the U.S. response
mainItems.summary: The sheer number of cases and deaths are a sign that the outbreak might have been smoldering before the virus was identified.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: The Outlander Series Finale Brings Jamie and Claire’s Love Story Full Circle
mainItems.summary: Love is a time loop in a finale that puts new meaning into “meant to be.” The post The Outlander Series Finale Brings Jamie and Claire’s Love Story Full Circle appeared first on Reactor .
mainItems.whyItMatters: On-theme for your cultural interests, especially when the concept is strong.

mainItems.title: The best everyday sandwich bread (that's secretly whole wheat!) : A recipe to make again and again.
mainItems.summary: Every baker needs a reliable sandwich bread recipe they can keep in their back pocket. Make a loaf and you’re halfway to a week full of toast, sandwiches, and afternoon PB&J pick-me-ups.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Lower-stakes, but high fit for the maker/craft side of your brain.

new.title: Scavengers Reign
new.why: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

new.title: Sugar
new.why: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

new.title: The Lazarus Project
new.why: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

new.title: Bodies
new.why: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

new.title: Constellation
new.why: A little more cerebral and mood-heavy, but it scratches the identity / reality-slippage itch.

new.title: The Devil’s Hour
new.why: Dark, twisty, and structurally ambitious without feeling like homework.

older.title: Counterpart
older.why: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

older.title: Patriot
older.why: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

older.title: Utopia
older.why: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

older.title: Rubicon
older.why: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

older.title: Continuum
older.why: A cleaner old-school sci-fi fit if you want procedural momentum wrapped around time-travel politics.

older.title: Person of Interest
older.why: Starts procedural and then quietly becomes one of the smartest AI / surveillance shows on television.

shared.title: Bad Sisters
shared.why: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

shared.title: Detectorists
shared.why: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

shared.title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
shared.why: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

shared.title: Somebody Somewhere
shared.why: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.

highlights.title: 12 unexpected sourdough bread recipes that aren't crusty loaves: From sourdough flatbread to sourdough milk bread — and even sourdough babka.
highlights.notes: Pinned from today's briefing, the kind of bread-nerd rabbit hole worth keeping.
